capabilities.rationale: Work-to-Cash Velocity: 4.0/5 over 55 reqs. Gated WO completion -> billing -> cash; close the 15-30 day billing lag; NTE alerts at 50/75/90%; eliminate revenue leak

capabilities.rationale: Acquisition Onboarding Speed: 4.0/5 over 51 reqs. Repeatable 6-8 week OpCo onboarding playbook; rapid data migration; configurable overlays for newly acquired entities.

agentic_future.rationale: [demo] Openness/data-access 4.0/5 (weighted 60%) over shipped AI 4.7/5 (40%): the platform's data access matters more than any single AI feature today. External research (cited): High - acquired TheLoops to launch 'IFS Loops' agentic AI with autonomous Digital Workers; IFS Cloud 25R2 ships agentic capabilities; IFS.ai Copilot for technicians.

vote.narrative: [demo vote] Recommend. Top-tier fit; advance to demos as a front-runner. IFS lands at 81.1/100 on the SSA category weighting (77.0/100 on the capability lens). Passes the Must gate. No architectural flags. Strongest where it counts: RLC 4.0/5, CXR 4.0/5, SCL 4.0/5. Weakest: PJE 3.0/5 (w15%), W2C 4.0/5 (w25%), TPA 4.0/5 (w20%). Best-fit OpCo archetypes: Residential / white-glove specialty 3.93/5, Small, low-funded / newly tucked-in (<$15M) 3.93/5; thinnest for Large, sophisticated, project-heavy multi-division 3.81/5, Large, process-mature service/controls leader 3.89/5. On the agentic future, 4.28/5, data-control risk Low — and remember, openness beats any single AI feature, because what we build on top is transient. Net: I'd recommend this one, and prove the rest in Charlotte.
vote.dissent: Counter-argument: requirement-level scoring rewards breadth of claims; a vendor that is narrower but deeper on Work-to-Cash and offline mobile could deliver more real value than this composite suggests. The demo and references should test depth, not just coverage.
top_risks: Project Execution & Financial Control weak at 3.0/5 (weight 15%).

external_research.name: IFS
external_research.product: IFS Cloud / IFS Field Service Management
external_research.ownership: Private, PE-backed (EQT + Hg, TA minority; ADIA & CPP joined Apr 2025). Valued >EUR 15B. >EUR 1.2B revenue 2024, >EUR 1B ARR, >30% YoY.
external_research.analyst: Leader in all 7 Gartner FSM MQs pre-retirement; only 2024 Gartner Peer Insights Customers' Choice for FSM.
external_research.hvac_fit: High - markets FSM for construction/HVAC (distributed teams, AI scheduling, job costing).
external_research.enterprise_union: High - natively unifies ERP+EAM+FSM on one platform (unique among the five); strong multi-entity. Certified-payroll/CBA specifics unverified - probe in RFP.
external_research.project_financials: Med - full ERP with project accounting/job costing; native AIA G702/G703 and ASC 606 WIP not confirmed publicly - verify.
external_research.agentic_ai: High - acquired TheLoops to launch 'IFS Loops' agentic AI with autonomous Digital Workers; IFS Cloud 25R2 ships agentic capabilities; IFS.ai Copilot for technicians.
external_research.risks: Breadth = complexity and heavier/costlier implementation; certified-payroll/CBA and AIA specifics need confirmation.

agentic_future.rationale: [demo] Openness/data-access 4.0/5 (weighted 60%) over shipped AI 4.7/5 (40%): the platform's data access matters more than any single AI feature today. External research (cited): High - Agentforce/Agentforce 360; Agentforce Field Service (Esri maps, Voice-to-Form); Data Cloud. Agentforce ARR +330% YoY (>$0.5B). Open platform/APIs.

vote.narrative: [demo vote] Disqualified. 5 unmet 'Must' requirement(s) — disqualifying per RFP Section 8. Salesforce lands at 74.1/100 on the SSA category weighting (63.6/100 on the capability lens). DISQUALIFIED — 5 unmet 'Must' requirement(s). Strongest where it counts: EVG 4.0/5, RLC 4.0/5, SCL 4.0/5. Weakest: PJE 1.85/5 (w15%), W2C 3.0/5 (w25%), TPA 3.0/5 (w20%). Best-fit OpCo archetypes: Strategic / National Accounts 3.38/5, Small, low-funded / newly tucked-in (<$15M) 3.43/5; thinnest for Large, sophisticated, project-heavy multi-division 3.25/5, Residential / white-glove specialty 3.35/5. On the agentic future, 4.28/5, data-control risk Low — and remember, openness beats any single AI feature, because what we build on top is transient. Net: I'd disqualified this one, and prove the rest in Charlotte.
vote.dissent: Counter-argument: requirement-level scoring rewards breadth of claims; a vendor that is narrower but deeper on Work-to-Cash and offline mobile could deliver more real value than this composite suggests. The demo and references should test depth, not just coverage.
top_risks: Project Execution & Financial Control weak at 1.85/5 (weight 15%).

external_research.name: Salesforce
external_research.ownership: Public mega-cap (NYSE: CRM). FY2026 revenue $41.5B; 34.1% non-GAAP op margin; record $15.0B operating cash flow; 21 straight years profitable.
external_research.analyst: Representative Vendor in the 2025 Gartner Market Guide for FSM.
external_research.hvac_fit: Med - generalist FSM, not trades-specific; lacks OOB trades/contractor templates.
external_research.enterprise_union: High - best-in-class enterprise scale/configurability, but capabilities come via configuration/customization, not prebuilt construction-labor features.
external_research.project_financials: Low - no native AIA/WIP/ASC 606; requires ERP integration or ISV add-ons.
external_research.agentic_ai: High - Agentforce/Agentforce 360; Agentforce Field Service (Esri maps, Voice-to-Form); Data Cloud. Agentforce ARR +330% YoY (>$0.5B). Open platform/APIs.
external_research.risks: Heavy integration/SI dependency (typical deployments 9-18 months, $300K-$800K implementation); no native project financials or certified payroll; multi-tenant SaaS vs. single-tenant requirement - confirm.

agentic_future.rationale: [demo] Openness/data-access 4.0/5 (weighted 60%) over shipped AI 2.85/5 (40%): the platform's data access matters more than any single AI feature today. External research (cited): Med - markets an 'AI-driven contractor platform'; investing Series C in API architecture (positive for openness), but agentic toolset less mature than Salesforce/IFS.

vote.narrative: [demo vote] Shortlist. Credible contender; advance to demos to close evidence gaps. BuildOps lands at 70.8/100 on the SSA category weighting (73.0/100 on the capability lens). Passes the Must gate. No architectural flags. Strongest where it counts: TPA 4.0/5, PJE 4.0/5, CXR 4.0/5. Weakest: ACQ 3.0/5 (w10%), EVG 3.0/5 (w10%), RLC 3.0/5 (w10%). Best-fit OpCo archetypes: Large, sophisticated, project-heavy multi-division 3.53/5, Residential / white-glove specialty 3.57/5; thinnest for Small, low-funded / newly tucked-in (<$15M) 3.49/5, Large, process-mature service/controls leader 3.51/5. On the agentic future, 3.54/5, data-control risk Low — and remember, openness beats any single AI feature, because what we build on top is transient. Net: I'd shortlist this one, and prove the rest in Charlotte.
vote.dissent: Counter-argument: requirement-level scoring rewards breadth of claims; a vendor that is narrower but deeper on Work-to-Cash and offline mobile could deliver more real value than this composite suggests. The demo and references should test depth, not just coverage.

external_research.name: BuildOps
external_research.ownership: Private, VC-backed. $127M Series C (Mar 2025) at $1B valuation (unicorn), led by Meritech; Bond Capital and Schneider Electric's SE Ventures participated; ~$225.8M total funding. Pre-profitability/private.
external_research.analyst: Newer entrant; no Gartner MQ/Market Guide or Peer Insights Customers' Choice placement found - a gap vs. incumbents.
external_research.hvac_fit: High - purpose-built for COMMERCIAL trades (HVAC, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, fire/life-safety); 'largest commercial trade contractor platform'; veteran-owned.
external_research.enterprise_union: Med - designed for large service contracts and multi-location mgmt; integrates with Sage/Vista/QuickBooks; markets union/prevailing-wage construction payroll. Depth at 60-OpCo/3,500-tech scale UNPROVEN - verify references.
external_research.project_financials: Med-High - project mgmt + on-site invoicing; ERP integrations (Vista/Sage) typically carry AIA/WIP. Native AIA G702/G703 + ASC 606 depth not confirmed - verify.
external_research.agentic_ai: Med - markets an 'AI-driven contractor platform'; investing Series C in API architecture (positive for openness), but agentic toolset less mature than Salesforce/IFS.
external_research.risks: Youngest and smallest; least proven at this buyer's scale; no analyst validation; single-tenant and multi-entity governance need confirmation; higher vendor-stability risk despite strong backing.

agentic_future.rationale: [demo] Openness/data-access 4.0/5 (weighted 60%) over shipped AI 2.85/5 (40%): the platform's data access matters more than any single AI feature today. External research (cited): Med - backed by PTC SLM/PLM 'digital thread'; specific ServiceMax agentic-AI products less prominent than peers - probe.

vote.narrative: [demo vote] Disqualified. 5 unmet 'Must' requirement(s) — disqualifying per RFP Section 8. ServiceMax lands at 74.1/100 on the SSA category weighting (63.6/100 on the capability lens). DISQUALIFIED — 5 unmet 'Must' requirement(s). Strongest where it counts: EVG 4.0/5, RLC 4.0/5, SCL 4.0/5. Weakest: PJE 1.85/5 (w15%), W2C 3.0/5 (w25%), TPA 3.0/5 (w20%). Best-fit OpCo archetypes: Strategic / National Accounts 3.38/5, Small, low-funded / newly tucked-in (<$15M) 3.43/5; thinnest for Large, sophisticated, project-heavy multi-division 3.25/5, Residential / white-glove specialty 3.35/5. On the agentic future, 3.54/5, data-control risk Low — and remember, openness beats any single AI feature, because what we build on top is transient. Net: I'd disqualified this one, and prove the rest in Charlotte.
vote.dissent: Counter-argument: requirement-level scoring rewards breadth of claims; a vendor that is narrower but deeper on Work-to-Cash and offline mobile could deliver more real value than this composite suggests. The demo and references should test depth, not just coverage.
top_risks: Project Execution & Financial Control weak at 1.85/5 (weight 15%).

external_research.name: ServiceMax
external_research.ownership: Owned by PTC (Nasdaq: PTC); acquired for $1.46B (Jan 2023). Ownership stable; PTC divested ThingWorx/Kepware (Mar 2026), NOT ServiceMax; treats ServiceMax as SLM 'anchor system of record'.
external_research.analyst: Long-standing recognized FSM vendor; listed in Gartner Peer Insights. No current MQ ranking.
external_research.hvac_fit: Med - asset-centric, OEM/equipment-oriented (asset hierarchies, SLAs, warranty, predictive maintenance), NOT contractor/trades/project-oriented.
external_research.enterprise_union: High for asset/contract mgmt at large service orgs; no evidence of native certified-payroll/CBA.
external_research.project_financials: Low - not a project-financials/AIA platform; asset-centric execution; AIA/WIP/ASC 606 needs external systems.
external_research.agentic_ai: Med - backed by PTC SLM/PLM 'digital thread'; specific ServiceMax agentic-AI products less prominent than peers - probe.
external_research.risks: Poor architectural fit (asset/OEM, not contractor/project); reported mobile (ServiceMax Go) sync reliability issues; identity tied to PTC PLM/asset strategy rather than mechanical contracting.

agentic_future.rationale: [demo] Openness/data-access 3.0/5 (weighted 60%) over shipped AI 3.55/5 (40%): the platform's data access matters more than any single AI feature today. External research (cited): Med-High capability (Titan Intelligence 'ti'; Atlas agentic sidekick) BUT API terms explicitly restrict using platform/data to train/benchmark AI - conflicts with customer-built-AI strategy.

vote.narrative: [demo vote] Shortlist. Credible contender; advance to demos to close evidence gaps. ServiceTitan lands at 70.8/100 on the SSA category weighting (73.0/100 on the capability lens). Passes the Must gate. No architectural flags. Strongest where it counts: TPA 4.0/5, PJE 4.0/5, CXR 4.0/5. Weakest: ACQ 3.0/5 (w10%), EVG 3.0/5 (w10%), RLC 3.0/5 (w10%). Best-fit OpCo archetypes: Large, sophisticated, project-heavy multi-division 3.53/5, Residential / white-glove specialty 3.57/5; thinnest for Small, low-funded / newly tucked-in (<$15M) 3.49/5, Large, process-mature service/controls leader 3.51/5. On the agentic future, 3.22/5, data-control risk Medium — and remember, openness beats any single AI feature, because what we build on top is transient. Net: I'd shortlist this one, and prove the rest in Charlotte.
vote.dissent: Counter-argument: requirement-level scoring rewards breadth of claims; a vendor that is narrower but deeper on Work-to-Cash and offline mobile could deliver more real value than this composite suggests. The demo and references should test depth, not just coverage.

external_research.name: ServiceTitan
external_research.ownership: Public (Nasdaq: TTAN) since Dec 2024; IPO ~$625M, ~$8.9B valuation. FY2025 revenue $771.9M (+26%); FY2026 guide ~$895-905M; approaching non-GAAP op profitability.
external_research.analyst: Dominant in residential/commercial trades software; no current Gartner MQ ranking.
external_research.hvac_fit: High - strong trades footprint (HVAC, plumbing, electrical); historically residential-trades-centric, expanding into commercial/construction.
external_research.enterprise_union: Med - 'Enterprise Hub' centralizes multi-location/franchise mgmt; union/prevailing-wage/certified payroll via Miter partnership (NOT native); multi-legal-entity at 60-OpCo scale to be validated.
external_research.project_financials: Med-High - notably strong for a trades platform: AIA-style G702/G703 pay apps, progress billing, schedule of values, retainage, change-order tracking.
external_research.agentic_ai: Med-High capability (Titan Intelligence 'ti'; Atlas agentic sidekick) BUT API terms explicitly restrict using platform/data to train/benchmark AI - conflicts with customer-built-AI strategy.
external_research.risks: AI/data-use API restrictions; residential/light-commercial roots (large multi-month commercial project depth less proven); payroll partner-dependent (Miter); multi-tenant SaaS vs single-tenant; 60-OpCo consolidation to stress-test.
